source_changes.detail: A retraction relation is now present (via crossref, openalex) that was absent before.
Re-resolved via crossref; no retraction/correction/supersession relation present. This holds the source is still clean as of this recheck - it does not prove nothing will ever change.
Could not be re-verified this pass (no resolvable identifier, or every adapter was offline/uncached). The prior status is carried forward, not re-checked.

claim_changes.note: Cites now-retracted source(s) S-004; verify.py raises this as a new blocking/major issue on recheck (see gate_after). The claim's evidence_status is not auto-edited - a human decides.

contradiction_changes.reason: no source referenced by this contradiction's claims changed class

_schema_notes: Written by `verify.py --recheck --write`. change_class is a closed enum: not_rechecked | unchanged | retracted | superseded | corrected | unavailable. not_rechecked (nothing was queryable, or every adapter was offline) is decided BEFORE any flag comparison, so a source that could not be reached is NEVER reported 'unchanged'. after_evidence_status always equals before_evidence_status - a recheck never auto-edits 03_claims.jsonl or 04_contradictions.json; it surfaces the conflict through gate_after and a human (or the next full run) decides. No confidence delta is ever fabricated; verify() computes both gates. tripwires_evaluated is present only if 08/decision_tripwires.json exists (omitted, never []); pivotal_claims_touched only if 07c/decision_criticality.json exists. This is a manual, point-in-time re-check ('what changed since'), not monitoring - nothing runs unless invoked.
